knowledge_components.name: Understand what a system of linear equations is and what it means to be a solution (connection to intersection and simultaneity).

complicators.name: A solution satisfies all equations at once — check by substituting into every equation.
Without solving, relate slopes and intercepts to solution types (equal slopes different intercepts → no solution; equal slopes equal intercepts → infinitely many).

knowledge_components.name: Solve systems of linear equations by graphing (connection between graphical intersection and algebraic solution).

complicators.name: Graph both equations in slope–intercept form and identify the intersection point as the solution understanding this represents values satisfying both equations.

Graph equations given in standard form (choosing to use intercepts or convert to slope-intercept form) and identify the intersection point as the intersection point as the solution that satisfies both equations.
Estimate solutions from graphs with non-integer intersections and understand limitations of graphical precision while checking reasonableness through substitution.
Identify special cases through graphing: parallel lines (no solution) and coincident lines (infinitely many solutions) connecting visual patterns to algebraic analysis.
Solve systems when one variable is already isolated by substituting the expression into the other equation.
Isolate a variable strategically then substitute to create a single-variable equation.
Add or subtract equations when coefficients are already opposites to eliminate one variable directly.
Multiply one equation by a constant to create opposite coefficients then eliminate through addition or subtraction.
Multiply both equations by different constants to align coefficients for elimination when needed.

knowledge_components.name: Use techniques strategically to make sense of and solve systems of equations

complicators.name: Reason about the number and nature of solutions using the structure of equations without fully solving, analyzing slopes in slope-intercept form or proportionality between equations.
Determine and employ a preferred strategy to find the solution to the system of equations.
